prediction.reasoning: Using a random walk model with drift for short-term price prediction. Based on typical semiconductor stock volatility (annual ~30-35%), daily volatility is approximately 2%. Over 6 days, this translates to ~4.9% volatility. Point estimate assumes no systematic drift over such a short period. Confidence intervals are constructed using normal distribution assumptions around current price, with widening intervals reflecting increased uncertainty at higher confidence levels.
